description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, rectangular solar panel array mounted on a robust metal framework. The solar panel surface is composed of a grid of photovoltaic cells, likely made from dark, reflective glass or silicon, and is supported by a lattice of metallic struts and beams. The structure is elevated above a patterned ground surface, providing clearance beneath the panel for airflow and maintenance. The supporting frame appears to be constructed from metal, possibly steel or aluminum, with diagonal and vertical supports for stability. The overall design is utilitarian, optimized for maximum sunlight exposure and structural integrity. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are present, and the installation is intended for outdoor urban environments such as parking lots, parks, or roadside areas.
description: A large, elevated solar panel array mounted on a sturdy metal frame, designed for outdoor urban energy generation. The structure features a grid of photovoltaic cells and a patterned base surface.
description_view_0: Side view showing the tilted solar panel array and the supporting metal framework, with the patterned ground surface visible beneath.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the full width of the solar panel array and the symmetrical arrangement of the supporting struts.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the panel tilt and the open space beneath the structure.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the solar panel array and the lattice structure of the metal supports.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metal lattice frame, likely steel or aluminum
attribute_array_configuration: single rectangular grid of approximately 1x8 panels
